lineage: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
That injuries and penalties of persecutions are not to be feared by us, because greater is the Lord to protect than the devil to assault.

source.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

title: That injuries and penalties of persecutions are not to be feared by us, because greater is the Lord to protect than the devil to assault.

source.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: That injuries and penalties of persecutions are not to be feared by us, because greater is the Lord to protect than the devil to assault.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: John, in his epistle, proves this, saying: “Greater is He who is in you than he that is in the world.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Also in the cxviith Psalm: “I will not fear what man can do unto me; the Lord is my helper.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And again: “These in chariots, and those in horses; but we will glory in the name of the Lord our God.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: They themselves are bound, and they have fallen; but we have risen up, and stand upright.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And even more strongly the Holy Spirit, teaching and showing that the army of the devil is not to be feared, and that, if the foe should declare war against us, our hope consists rather in that war itself; and that by that conflict the righteous attain to the reward of the divine abode and eternal salvation,—lays down in the twenty-sixth Psalm, and says: “Though an host should be arrayed against me, my heart shall not fear; though war should rise up against me, in that will I put my hope.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: One hope have I sought of the Lord, this will I require; that I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Also in Exodus, the Holy Scripture declares that we are rather multiplied and increased by afflictions, saying: “And the more they afflicted them, so much the more they became greater, and waxed stronger.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And in the Apocalypse, divine protection is promised to our sufferings.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: “Fear nothing of these things,” it says, “which thou shalt suffer.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Nor does any one else promise to us security and protection, than He who also speaks by Isaiah the prophet, saying: “Fear not; for I have redeemed thee, and called thee by thy name: thou art mine.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And if thou passest through the water, I am with thee, and the rivers shall not overflow thee.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And if thou passest through the fire, thou shalt not be burned, and the flame shall not burn thee; for I, the Lord thy God, the Holy One of Israel, am He who maketh thee safe.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Who also promises in the Gospel that divine help shall not be wanting to God’s servants in persecutions, saying: “But when they shall deliver you up, take no thought how or what ye shall speak.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: For it shall be given you in that hour what ye shall speak.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: For it is not ye who speak, but the Spirit of your Father who speaketh in you.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And again: “Settle it in your hearts not to meditate before how to answer.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: For I will give you a mouth and wisdom, which your adversaries shall not be able to resist.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: As in Exodus God speaks to Moses when he delayed and trembled to go to the people, saying: “Who hath given a mouth to man? and who hath made the stammerer? and who the deaf man? and who the seeing, and the blind man?

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Have not I, the Lord God?

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And now go, and I will open thy mouth, and will instruct thee what thou shalt say.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Nor is it difficult for God to open the mouth of a man devoted to Himself, and to inspire constancy and confidence in speech to His confessor; since in the book of Numbers He made even a she-ass to speak against the prophet Balaam.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Wherefore in persecutions let no one think what danger the devil is bringing in, but let him indeed consider what help God affords; nor let human mischief overpower the mind, but let divine protection strengthen the faith; since every one, according to the Lord’s promises and the deservings of his faith, receives so much from God’s help as he thinks that he receives.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Nor is there anything which the Almighty is not able to grant, unless the failing faith of the receiver be deficient and give way.
